_exempt: <!-- PRIMARY-SOURCE-EXEMPT: reason=QA-MEM Phase 4.8 item 6 Track C Schumann primary-source corpus + Williams-1992-substitutes extension. 4 SourceWorks + 15 verbatim SourceClaims: Schumann 1952 (6 German claims from schumann_1952_excerpts.md) + Price 2016 + Bozóki/Sátori/Williams 2021 + Ikeda 2018 (9 English claims from schumann_williams_substitutes_excerpts.md) grounding Earth Q=4-6, ΔQ/Q=0.12-0.15 solar-cycle modulation, ΔI/I≤0.60 SR-intensity modulation, and 2003 single-event X-ray/SPE response at low-latitude Kuju. Domain=physics. Do not edit quote fields. -->
description: Schumann primary-source claims corpus. Schumann 1952 provides the canonical original derivation for the Earth-ionosphere cavity eigenfrequency (≈ 11 Hz theoretical) and the closed-form √(n(n+1)) harmonic series. Williams-1992-substitutes extension (acquired 2026-04-22 after Williams 1992 Science 256:1184 was captured DEFERRED AAAS-paywalled in commit 3bde4e4) adds: Price 2016 (Atmosphere, Q=4-6 canonical scalar + 8/14/20/26 Hz observed series), Bozóki/Sátori/Williams 2021 (Frontiers Earth Sci, ΔQ/Q=0.12-0.15 solar-cycle + ΔI/I≤0.60 intensity modulation, Williams-lineage co-authorship), Ikeda 2018 (E3S, direct 2003 solar X-ray/SPE SR-frequency event data at Kuju). Together these ground the future qa_heartmath_mapping_cert_v1 Schumann-harmonic ↔ orbit-harmonic-ratio mapping. Thread 3 of OB 2026-03-25 research-agenda. PDFs on disk under Documents/schumann_resonance/; anchors in docs/theory/schumann_1952_excerpts.md and docs/theory/schumann_williams_substitutes_excerpts.md.

works.title: Schumann (1952) — Über die strahlungslosen Eigenschwingungen einer leitenden Kugel, die von einer Luftschicht und einer Ionosphärenhülle umgeben ist
works.body: Winfried Otto Schumann, Elektrophysikalisches Institut der Technischen Hochschule München. Zeitschrift für Naturforschung 7a:149-154, 1952; received 1 October 1951. DOI 10.1515/zna-1952-0202. The canonical original derivation of the Earth-ionosphere cavity resonances invoked throughout the HeartMath / McCraty / Oschman literature as 7.83 Hz. Derives a closed-form lowest eigenfrequency ω_ei ≈ √2·c/R ≈ 70 rad/s (f ≈ 11 Hz) for Earth dimensions, excited by lightning, and a theoretical harmonic-series law m_n = √(n(n+1)). PDF: 5.1MB, 6pp, retrieved 2026-04-20 via degruyterbrill.com/document/doi/10.1515/zna-1952-0202/pdf.

works.title: Price (2016) — ELF Electromagnetic Waves from Lightning: The Schumann Resonances
works.body: Colin Price, Department of Geosciences, Tel Aviv University. Atmosphere 7(9):116, 2016. DOI 10.3390/atmos7090116. MDPI CC-BY. Canonical modern review of Schumann resonance physics. Carries the load-bearing primary-source scalar Q=4-6 for the Earth-ionosphere cavity (citing Jones 1974), observed harmonic series 8/14/20/26 Hz, and Lorentzian-fit spectral decomposition methodology. Picked as a Williams-1992-substitute for the review-scope numerical content when Williams 1992 Science 256:1184 was captured DEFERRED AAAS-paywalled in commit 3bde4e4. PDF: 4.0MB, 20pp, retrieved 2026-04-22 via pdfs.semanticscholar.org/765e/0527e9d0f264efb1fd530cf5fe79b191f692.pdf.

works.title: Bozóki, Sátori, Williams et al. (2021) — Solar Cycle-Modulated Deformation of the Earth–Ionosphere Cavity
works.body: Tamás Bozóki, Gabriella Sátori, Earle R. Williams, Irina Mironova, Péter Steinbach, Emma C. Bland, Alexander Koloskov, Yuri M. Yampolski, Oleg V. Budanov, Mariusz Neska, Ashwini K. Sinha, Rahul Rawat, Mitsuteru Sato, Ciaran D. Beggan, Sergio Toledo-Redondo, Yakun Liu, Robert Boldi. Frontiers in Earth Science 9:689127, 2021. DOI 10.3389/feart.2021.689127. Frontiers CC-BY (GOLD). Earle Williams (MIT Parsons Lab) is a co-author, making this paper a direct Williams-lineage successor to the 1992 Science paper — the strongest single substitute in the literature for the Williams-1992 numerical scope. Carries the quantitative core: ΔQ/Q=0.12-0.15 long-term solar-cycle Q-factor modulation (citing Sátori et al. 2005), ΔI/I≤0.60 SR intensity solar-cycle modulation at Antarctic stations, and ΔQ/Q=0.10-0.30 event-scale modulation from EEP events at Hornsund. PDF: 5.5MB, 20pp, retrieved 2026-04-22 via frontiersin.org/articles/10.3389/feart.2021.689127/pdf.

works.title: Ikeda, Uozumi, Yoshikawa, Fujimoto, Abe (2018) — Schumann resonance parameters at Kuju station during solar flares
works.body: Akihiro Ikeda (National Institute of Technology, Kagoshima College), Teiji Uozumi, Akimasa Yoshikawa, Shuji Abe (International Center for Space Weather Science and Education, Kyushu University), Akiko Fujimoto (Kyushu Institute of Technology). E3S Web of Conferences 62:01012, 2018 (Solar-Terrestrial Relations and Physics of Earthquake Precursors). DOI 10.1051/e3sconf/20186201012. EDP Sciences CC-BY. Direct primary-source 2003 solar X-ray / SPE event data at low-latitude Kuju station (M.Lat. 23.4°N). Explicit measurement protocol: induction magnetometer, 50 Hz sampling, 10-s PSD windows, FFT, 6.0–9.0 Hz f_1 peak-search range, GOES-10 X-ray/proton reference. Asymmetric response: SR frequency in H increases during X-ray enhancement, D decreases during SPEs. Picked as a Williams-1992-substitute for single-event solar-cycle-shift numerical content; explicitly cites Roldugin 1999 and extends the same class of measurement to low latitudes at CC-BY venue. PDF: 1.9MB, 4pp, retrieved 2026-04-22 via e3s-conferences.org/articles/e3sconf/pdf/2018/37/e3sconf_strpep2018_01012.pdf.

claims.quote: Über die strahlungslosen Eigenschwingungen einer leitenden Kugel, die von einer Luftschicht und einer Ionosphärenhülle umgeben ist. Von W. 0. SCHUMANN. AVIS dem Elektrophysikalischen Institut der Technischen Hochschule München. (Z. Naturforschg. 7a, 149-154 [1952]; eingegangen am 1. Oktober 1951)
claims.title: Schumann 1952 (p.149): full title / author / journal citation

claims.quote: Die tiefste Eigenfrequenz ungedämpfter Schwingungen ωei liegt zwischen Null und ωei = √2 c/r je nach der relativen Dicke der Luftschicht und der Plasmaeigenfrequenz ω₀. Für irdische Dimensionen liegt ωei nahe dem oberen Wert und ergibt eine Frequenz von ≈ 11 Per/sec.
claims.title: Schumann 1952 (p.149, abstract): ωei between 0 and √2·c/r; Earth dimensions give ≈ 11 Hz

claims.quote: Setzen wir für die Erde R = 6000 km und c = 300 000 km/sec, so ist ωei = 50 √2 ≈ 70 sec⁻¹ und die sekundliche Frequenz f_ei max ≈ 11 sec⁻¹. Durch eine stoßartige Erregung dieses 'Hohlraumes', z. B. durch eine Blitzentladung, sind als tiefste erregte Schwingungen Frequenzen dieser Größe zu erwarten.
claims.title: Schumann 1952 (p.152): Earth R=6000km, c=300000km/s → ωei=50√2≈70 s⁻¹, f_ei_max≈11 Hz, lightning excitation

claims.quote: Für ma' → ∞ ist m₁ = √(n(n+1)) = ωei R/c. Es sind dies die zonalen Oberschwingungen geringer Ordnung für 'sehr große Plasmadichte' R/c · ω₀ » 1, aber auch zugleich die Oberschwingungen einer von zwei unendlich guten Leitern begrenzten Kugelschale aus Luft.
claims.title: Schumann 1952 (p.153): closed-form harmonic series m_n = √(n(n+1)) = ωei·R/c for large plasma density

claims.quote: Der berechneten Frequenz von f_ei = 11 sec⁻¹ entspricht eine Vakuumwellenlänge freier Wellen von λ_ei = 27 300 km.
claims.title: Schumann 1952 (p.153): f_ei=11 Hz ↔ λ_ei = 27 300 km vacuum wavelength

claims.quote: Die Winkelgeschwindigkeit dieser Wellen längs des Kugelumfangs ist dθ/dt = ω und die Lineargeschwindigkeit v = R dθ/dt = ω R. Für ωei = √2 c/R wird v = √2 c.
claims.title: Schumann 1952 (p.153): surface phase velocity v = ωR; at ωei = √2·c/R, v = √2·c

claims.quote: The dimensionless quality factor Q of the resonant cavity may be determined as a ratio between the stored energy and the energy loss per cycle. Considering only the electrically stored energy [34]: Q = Re S / (2 Im S) (4). On Earth, the resonance is characterized by a quality factor Q ranging from 4 to 6 [35].
claims.title: Price 2016 (p.3, after Eq.4): canonical Earth Q-factor Q=4-6 (citing Jones 1974)

claims.quote: SR modes can usually be observed in the frequency domain at 8 Hz, 14 Hz, 20 Hz, 26 Hz, etc. (Figure 4b). For studies of global lightning activity, the SR spectra are normally fitted to a set of Lorentzian curves [54,57,58] where the curve for each mode is described by three parameters: peak amplitude, peak frequency and the quality factor.
claims.title: Price 2016 (p.5): observed harmonic series 8/14/20/26 Hz + Lorentzian-fit methodology

claims.quote: SR parameters have been shown to vary during cosmic gamma-ray bursts [177,178], solar flares and solar proton events [62,178–180], as well as over the 11-year solar cycle [181–183].
claims.title: Price 2016 (p.14): canonical textbook statement of SR solar-cycle, flare, and SPE modulation

claims.quote: SR intensity is an excellent indicator of lightning activity and its distribution on global scales. However, long-term measurements from high latitude SR stations revealed a pronounced in-phase solar cycle modulation of SR intensity seemingly contradicting optical observations of lightning from satellite, which do not show any significant solar cycle variation in the intensity and spatial distribution of lightning activity on the global scale.
claims.title: Bozóki 2021 (p.1, abstract): SR-intensity solar-cycle modulation vs. lightning-activity absence — motivates cavity-deformation mechanism

claims.quote: The height and scale height of the lower (electric) layer were considered to be invariant over the solar cycle while for the upper (magnetic) layer the authors inferred a height decrease of 4.7 km and a scale height decrease from 6.9 to 4.2 km for the first SR mode from solar maximum to solar minimum. These changes result in an increase of the resonator's Q-factor by 12–15 % (Sátori et al., 2005). The intensity of a damped simple harmonic oscillator is known to be proportional to the square of its Q-factor (Feynman et al., 1963), in agreement with the theoretical description of SR (Nelson, 1967, Eqs 2–24; Williams et al., 2006). It follows that a 12–15 % change in the Q-factor of the Earth–ionosphere cavity can result in a 25–30 % increase of the first SR mode's intensity.
claims.title: Bozóki 2021 (p.13, §4.1): ΔQ/Q=0.12-0.15 long-term solar-cycle modulation + causal chain from upper-layer height decrease

claims.quote: In 2015 long-term SR intensity records from the Ukrainian Antarctic station 'Akademik Vernadsky' were published which clearly showed a pronounced solar cycle modulation (up to 60 %) of SR intensity (Nickolaenko et al., 2015), just as in an earlier paper by Füllekrug et al. (2002) using data from the Antarctic station Arrival Heights.
claims.title: Bozóki 2021 (p.2, intro): ΔI/I≤0.60 solar-cycle SR-intensity modulation at Antarctic stations (Vernadsky + Arrival Heights)

claims.quote: SR intensity clearly increases during the event not only in the HNS component at Syowa (so near the radar) but in the Northern Hemisphere under daytime conditions at the Hornsund (HRN) station as well where increased Q-factor values (by ∼10–30 %) can be observed during the event. The relative increase of SR intensity is as large as 50–100 % at Syowa during this event.
claims.title: Bozóki 2021 (p.12, §3.5): event-scale ΔQ/Q=0.10-0.30 from EEP event at Hornsund station

claims.quote: We examined the Schumann resonance (SR) at low-latitude station KUJ by comparing with solar X-ray flux and solar proton flux at a geostationary orbit. For intense solar activity in October-November 2003, the reaction of the SR frequency to X-ray enhancement and SPEs was different. The SR frequency in H component increased at the time of the X-ray enhancement. The response of SR seems to be caused by the increase of the electron density in the ionospheric D region which ionized by the enhanced solar X-ray flux. In the case of the SPEs, the SR frequency in D component decreased with enhancement of solar proton flux.
claims.title: Ikeda 2018 (p.1, abstract): asymmetric SR-frequency response — H up on X-ray, D down on SPE

claims.quote: We examined the fundamental mode of SR observed by an induction magnetometer at Kuju, Japan (M.Lat. = 23.4 degree, M. Lon. = 201.0 degree). The observation is a part of activities by International Center for Space Weather Science and Education, Kyushu University and the observation of the induction magnetometer started in 2003. The components of ground magnetic field used in this study are horizontal northward component (H) and horizontal eastward component (D). Sampling rate of the observation data is 50 Hz. We identified frequency of the SR by calculating PSD (power spectral density) every 10 seconds segment by using FFT (First Fourier Transformation). Generally, frequency of the fundamental mode of SR is about 7.8 Hz [5], we therefore found a peak frequency ranging 6.0 Hz to 9.0 Hz. This peak frequency is denoted by SR frequency. To compare with SR frequency, solar X-ray (0.05-0.3 nm) and proton flux (40-80 MeV) were also analyzed. These data were obtained by the GOES-10 satellite on a geostationary orbit.
claims.title: Ikeda 2018 (p.2, §2): measurement protocol — induction mag, 50 Hz sampling, 10-s PSD/FFT, 6.0-9.0 Hz f_1 band, GOES-10 reference

claims.quote: The enhancement of solar X-ray flux occurred on 18 October, 2003 and lasted for about 19 days before it recovered to a previous level. The SR frequency in H component followed the variation of the X-ray flux (see black arrows in Figure 1). The effect on SR frequency is not related to the local time at the observatory, since a high degree of the SR frequency in H component continued during the enhancement of solar X-ray flux. The effect on SR seems to be the global character.
claims.title: Ikeda 2018 (p.2, §3): 2003-10-18 19-day X-ray enhancement event + global-character claim
